approach: HITL for barriers (mission brief) — emit verdict packet and STOP at both gates; never self-assert past a barrier gate

Carve A barrier-mission handling — formation calls out a project-wide escalation as a barrier; mission-graph models the fence and ready hoists it early, never offering it as a normal node-owning Mission

Carve B (star) blast-field auto-compute — NEW top-level blast-estimate node + engine computing artifact-count x centrality x sensitivity from a touch-set, sharpening the hand-asserted blast: field additively
Lowering: op5 is authored coarse (0/1); this CR lowers op5-m1 into two carves shipped as one PR — the #191 F1+F2 precedent
Prior art guard: ssa-lowering (#210) already grades barrier detection at lowering time; this CR builds the formation-source + scheduler-fence half only

why: Op5 (#192), the sole mission-graph frontier node op5-m1. Delivers TWO of Op5's three parts. (1) NEW behavioral node sdd/blast-estimate (concept orchestration) -- the star deliverable: a read-only estimator consuming a touch-set (declared pre-work, or corrected post-work by touch-set-correction #199) and computing blast as artifact-count x centrality(dependency fan-in) x sensitivity(declared in opt-in .agents/sdd/sensitive-paths.toml), exactly per design/autonomy-rubric.md Testability-harness; lines the computed level up against the hand-asserted blast: as agrees/under-called/over-called, mirroring touch-set-correction's confirmed/missed/over-declared. Reports-never-writes (the graph's single writer records it) -- same boundary as touch-set-correction and resolve-governances. Both rubric exclusions held by scenarios: NOT compatibility/breakage, NOT surface location. Placed as a top-level sibling mirroring touch-set-correction/collision-ladder/ssa-lowering. Scope discipline: NO mission-graph change needed -- blast is already a free string defaulting to unknown, and schema-v1 additive tolerance is an existing frozen scenario. (2) formation barrier CALL-OUT -- additive on the @frozen formation.feature (5 added / 0 modified / 0 removed via gherkin-cli structural diff, so it SELF-CLEARS, stays @frozen, no re-open, no Clearance floor): a project-wide escalation is called out explicitly as a barrier, states hoist-early, names the project it fences, escalates on reach alone regardless of contract impact; a node-scoped finding does not. README names the formation<->ssa-lowering seam (formation calls out when PRODUCING a fence at escalation; ssa-lowering #210 recognizes when RECEIVING a CR at lowering -- both design-sanctioned, not duplicates). SCOPE CUT, owner-directed: Op5's third part, the mission-graph scheduler FENCE, failed 3 consecutive cold-judge rounds (scenarios 10->14->17, contradictions 1->1->3; rounds 2-3 each refuted the scenarios added to fix the prior round). Root cause was a METHOD error -- the fence was specified BY EXAMPLE with no stated fold rule, and pairwise Given overlap grows quadratically. mission-graph/ reverted byte-identical to main; fence re-filed as #224 carrying the closed-form rule R'' + its induction proof of termination + the 3 counterexample snapshots, to be designed RULE-FIRST. Judge round 5 ALIGNED true (oracle/builder/architect all PASS, no open markers, nothing to fix; it called the cut 'the Oracle bar working, not scope damage'). Rounds 1-4 each found REAL defects, notably a permanent INVISIBLE scheduler wedge (a fold-computed fence leaves the graph acyclic, so cycles reports a wedged project HEALTHY) and a frozen-scenario contradiction that the addOnly title-keyed diff was structurally blind to. MEDIUM blast; leash auto-none, HITL for barriers -- human-ratified in-session by unional, never self-asserted. This mission closes #192 and spawns #224.
